text:
Your pain is real. Breakups hurt because something that mattered is now gone, and your feelings about that are valid.

Here is what I know: the sharp ache you feel right now will not stay this sharp. That is not a dismissal of your pain. It is a fact about how pain works. The intensity softens. Not because the relationship did not matter, but because you are built to adapt and continue.

Some things worth saying plainly. You do not need to figure out right now whether you made mistakes or whether they did. That accounting can wait. Right now your only job is to get through today. Eat something. Sleep when you can. Let people who care about you be nearby if that helps.

Do not make big decisions. Do not send messages you will regret. Do not try to figure out what the relationship meant or what comes next. None of that is urgent.

What is true: you had real experiences with this person. Those were yours. The ending does not erase them or mean they were wrong to have. Something can be good and still end.

You will have moments in the coming days where it lifts a little and moments where it hits hard again. Both are normal. The pattern is not linear.

You are not behind. You are not broken. You are someone going through a hard thing, which is different.

The future is genuinely open in ways you cannot see clearly right now. That openness is not a threat. It will eventually feel like possibility.

For now, just get through today. That is enough.
